experiment: End-to-end self-improvement under the live (mock) manifold (ADR-103)
method: evolve() in sandboxMode 'mock' over the graduated context-gated ladder (DEFAULT_MOCK_TASKS), 12 generations × 6 children, seed 11, crossover+epistasis, promotionDelta 0.001. Best-so-far finalScore tracked per generation. Deterministic/reproducible (no LLM, no wall-clock).
conclusion: Under the live manifold the loop genuinely self-improves: it evolves the contextBuilder surface (window 30->70) to solve more rungs of the ladder, climbing finalScore 0.765 -> 0.985 and reaching the gate ceiling by generation 3.
honest_caveats: Mock mode: a deterministic simulated agent loop on surface PARAMETERS, not a real LLM coding task (Tier 2 deferred).
Climb is via context-window widening (the surface that mutates upward); the retry budget does NOT mutate upward past baseline {1,2,3} — a DeterministicMutator limitation found during this run.
This smooth ladder does not differentiate selection strategies (clade vs behavioral-diversity reach the ceiling identically); a deceptive/epistatic landscape would be needed to separate them.
Earlier deceptive (retry+context coupled) and retry-gated ladders did NOT climb (greedy promotion cannot cross an epistatic plateau, and retry never mutates up) — reported honestly.
